rulings.Case Title: DAVID ASTLE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, April 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary adjudication as to issue 2 (strict liability) is granted. A contractor defendant who installs defective products is not strictly liable for injuries caused by the products. (Monte Vista Development Corp. v. Superior Court (1991) 226 Cal.App.3d 1681.) Plaintiff¿s response to Defendant¿s interrogatory seeking all facts that Defendant is strictly liable contains no information that Defendant ever sold, manufactured or supplied any asbestos containing product. Plaintiff¿s evidence in opposition does not create a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff¿s request for a continuance is denied. The remaining issues raised by Defendant¿s motion are moot. Defendant has withdrawn its motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication on issues 1 (negligence) and 4 (premises owner/contractor liability). Plaintiff has dismissed the causes of action that are the subjects of issues 3 (false representation) and 5 (punitive damages). =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Fourth Cause Of Action And Prayer For Punitive Damages From The First Amended Complaint For Damages
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, April 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Fisher Scientific Company L.L.C.¿s motion to strike plaintiff¿s fourth cause of action and prayer for punitive damages from the first amended complaint for damages is overruled. Plaintiff¿s complaint sufficiently alleges a claim for punitive damages and sets forth the facts upon which the claim is premised. Plaintiff alleges that defendants, including Fisher Scientific, have known of the health hazards of asbestos since at least 1933 and concealed information regarding these hazards from the general public. =(220/HEK)
